overview: Temur Prowess aims to cast a stream of cheap, efficient noncreature spells to trigger Prowess, then attack with a single creature to deal massive burst damage. It excels at racing slower control, combo, and midrange decks, but must close games quickly before opponents can stabilize or answer its threats.
win_conditions: A fully pumped creature (3+ Prowess triggers) attacking for 5–7 damage.
Win quickly by chaining cheap spells into a single attacker.
Use Stormchaser’s Talent or Elusive Otter to draw into more triggers if needed.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2–3 lands, at least one Prowess trigger spell (e.g., Stormchaser’s Talent, Elusive Otter, or cheap instants like Lightning Strike/Opt), and a creature to attack with.
Prioritize hands with 2–3 mana sources and 2+ spells.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, 0 spells, or only high-cost spells that can’t trigger Prowess early.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cheap spell (Opt, Lightning Strike, or similar) to set up Prowess and draw/filter.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast another spell, then attack with a creature (e.g., Slickshot Show-Off or a 2/2 body) for 2–3 damage.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast colored spells and tap for combat.
Always leave open mana for protection or counterspells if possible.
mid_game: Avoid overcommitting to casting spells if you cannot attack with a creature.
Save mana for protection, counters, or removal.
Manage hand size by using draw spells (Elusive Otter) only when you have a creature ready to attack.
Don’t waste Prowess triggers on creatures that can’t attack or will die to removal.
Commit threats when you have interaction (counters, removal, or discard) to protect them.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, heavy interaction, or a clear win condition.
Prowess is about timing the pump
don’t trigger Prowess on a creature that will be blocked or removed before combat.
late_game: A fully pumped creature (3+ Prowess triggers) attacking for 5–7 damage.
Win quickly by chaining cheap spells into a single attacker.
Use Stormchaser’s Talent or Elusive Otter to draw into more triggers if needed.
Go all-in when you have removal/counters to protect your attacker, when the opponent is at low life (≤5), or when you can close the game before they can stabilize.
Don’t hold back if you have a clear path to victory.

sequencing_tips: Common mistakes include overcasting spells without attacking, mismanaging mana for colors, and triggering Prowess on creatures that can’t attack.
Use Elusive Otter to draw into more triggers, but only when you have a creature ready to attack.
Stormchaser’s Talent can pump and draw, but be careful of timing.
always ask yourself if you can attack after casting a spell.

matchups.advice: Use cheap spells (Opt, Lightning Strike) to force counters, then attack with a pumped creature. Sequence spells to bait counters, and use uncounterable threats if available. Don’t overcommit to casting multiple spells if the opponent has multiple counterspells.
Apply early pressure to prevent them from stabilizing. Use targeted removal on key ramp creatures. Don’t let them get ahead in mana; Prowess wins by racing, not outlasting.
Use removal and counterspells to stall and survive the early game. Prowess is slow against fast aggro; prioritize survival, then close the game with a fully pumped attacker once you stabilize.

Pressure early and force interaction. Use discard or cheap spells to disrupt their plan, then attack with pumped creatures. Don’t let them stabilize; keep the pressure on and win before they can draw their answer.

Win the spell war. Use counterspells, discard, and efficient removal to disrupt their Prowess triggers. Prioritize protecting your own triggers and attacking with a single creature. Control the board by forcing them to use interaction on your spells.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God), and targeted removal.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or have no interaction left.
Track graveyard hate if relevant, and monitor life totals to know when to go all-in or play defensively.
